<<Construct SQL commands in MySQL, tailored specifically to eliminate all records bearing the "Inactive" status, from a table carrying the distinct name 'users'>>  
<<Then, create a transaction log, tracking these modifications with a timestamp and altering the user’s status on a separate 'status_log' table>>  
<<The 'status_log' table should have the following structure:>>  
<<user_id, status, modification_date>>  
<<Additionally, implement error handling to return any possible errors or exceptions during the deletion process>>  
<<Lastly, this must be created as a stored procedure that can be called upon as needed>>  

<<Create a stored procedure named 'delete_inactive_users' that deletes all records from 'users' where status = 'Inactive'>>  
<<Begin a transaction to ensure data integrity during the deletion process>>  
<<For each record deleted, insert a row into 'status_log' with the user_id, status, and modification_date>>  
<<Commit the transaction to finalize the deletion and log entries>>  
<<Handle any errors that may occur during the deletion process, such as constraint violations or duplicate entries>>  
<<The procedure will return an error message if any of the operations fail>>  

<<The 13-th sentence is:>>  
<<The procedure will return an error message if any of the operations fail>>  
<<The 3-th word of this sentence is:>>  
<<fail>>  

<<The palindrome in the response is:>>  
<<A man, a plan, a canal: Panama>>  

<<total>>
